user.title: Shelly Cloud DIY
user.description: Connect Home Assistant to the Shelly Cloud. Open the Shelly App → User settings → Authorization cloud key → GET KEY to obtain both values below.

data.local_gateway_url: Local gateway URL (optional, for EM historical data)

data_description.auth_key: The long string shown under Authorization cloud key in the Shelly App.
data_description.server_uri: The hostname shown below the key, e.g. shelly-42-eu.shelly.cloud.
data_description.poll_interval: How often Home Assistant polls Shelly Cloud for state. Default 5 s; range 3–60 s. Shelly enforces a 1 req/s rate limit.
data_description.local_gateway_url: Only needed if you use a local Shelly gateway to back-fill historical energy data.

devices.title: Choose devices to expose
devices.description: The Shelly Cloud account exposes {total} device(s). Pick a bulk action above, or adjust the list below directly. If every device is ticked on save, future devices added to the Shelly account will be auto-enabled too. Offline devices are marked with ⚠.

data_description.bulk_action: "Tick all" or "Untick all" overrides the manual list below on save. Pick "Manual selection" to keep the list as-is.
data_description.enabled_devices: Only ticked devices produce Home Assistant entities.

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate Shelly Cloud DIY
reauth_confirm.description: The Shelly Cloud rejected the stored Authorization cloud key. Paste a fresh key from the Shelly App (User settings → Authorization cloud key) to restore access.

error.cannot_connect: Could not reach Shelly Cloud. Check the server URI and your internet connection.
error.invalid_auth: Shelly Cloud rejected the Authorization cloud key.
error.invalid_gateway_url: Local gateway URL must be http(s) and must not point at localhost.
error.unknown: Unexpected error — see Home Assistant logs.

abort.already_configured: This Shelly account is already configured.
abort.reauth_entry_missing: Could not find the integration to re-authenticate.

init.title: Shelly Cloud DIY options
init.description: Adjust how often the integration polls Shelly Cloud for state and, optionally, the local gateway URL used by the historical-data service. Device selection follows on the next step.

data.local_gateway_url: Local gateway URL (optional, for EM historical data)

data_description.poll_interval: 3–60 s. Lower values feel snappier but consume more of Shelly's 1 req/s rate-limit budget.
data_description.local_gateway_url: Only needed if you use a local Shelly gateway to back-fill historical energy data.

devices.title: Change device selection
devices.description: The Shelly Cloud account exposes {total} device(s). Pick a bulk action above, or adjust the list below directly. If every device is ticked on save, future devices will be auto-enabled too.

data_description.bulk_action: "Tick all" or "Untick all" overrides the manual list below on save.
data_description.enabled_devices: Only ticked devices produce Home Assistant entities.

error.invalid_gateway_url: Local gateway URL must be http(s) and must not point at localhost.

download_and_convert_history.name: Download and convert historical energy data
download_and_convert_history.description: Fetches CSV exports from the local Shelly gateway and imports them into Home Assistant's long-term statistics.

gateway_url.name: Gateway URL
gateway_url.description: Base URL of the local gateway (overrides the one configured in options).

device_id.name: Device ID
device_id.description: Optional — restrict the import to a single EM device. All EM devices are imported if omitted.
